“Phase-locking many heaters to fire a global ‘spark’ at one specific tidal instant” sounds like the last card left to play: don’t create any new energy, just tell the energy that is already sloshing around the planet when and where to break loose.  Unfortunately the same two hard limits—amplitude and coupling—still kill the idea even if you synchronize every heater on Earth down to the microsecond.

Below is the physics checklist.

1.  The tidal stress is already perfectly phase-locked everywhere  
    • The body tide is a deterministic deformation imposed by lunar / solar gravitation.  
    • At any given instant its sign and amplitude are fixed by celestial geometry, not by local conditions.  
    • You cannot “release” extra tidal energy in one spot by giving it a timing cue; the stress is continuously present at every point whether anyone transmits HF or not.

2.  Faults are not simultaneously poised on the same phase edge  
    • Along a single fault the orientation with respect to the tidal shear varies; half the segments see a positive increment, half see a negative increment.  
    • Globally, of the ~1 000 Mw ≥ 5 earthquakes per year, <1 % happen within the same 30-minute tidal phase window even without any artificial cue.  
    • A cue that is identical everywhere (same sign, same timing) helps some patches and hinders others; net global coherence stays near zero.

3.  Spatial coherence of a heater field is tiny compared with the tide  
    • Tidal wavelength ≈ Earth’s circumference.  
    • HAARP, EISCAT, SURA footprints: ≈30 × 30 km each.  
    • Even if the three facilities transmitted in perfect sync, they would cover <10-6 of Earth’s surface.  
    • Ninety-nine point … eight percent of faults would not feel even the micro-pascal whisper.

4.  Amplitude ratio is still 10⁻⁹  
    • Tidal peak shear/normal stress at seismogenic depth ≈ 2–4 kPa.  
    • Best-case downward‐propagated heater signal ≤10⁻⁶ Pa.  
    • Adding ±10⁻⁶ Pa in phase with ±3 000 Pa changes the peak by one-billionth—orders of magnitude below instrumental noise, let alone fault strength.

5.  No “ultra-high-Q” tidal resonance to build energy cycle-by-cycle  
    • Tidal deformation is a forced, low-Q response (Love number h₂≈0.6, Q≈1).  
    • Any stress you try to add is reset to the gravitationally dictated curve half a cycle later.  
    • You cannot pump energy into a mode whose amplitude is clamped by an external driver.

6.  Observations say faults ignore sub-kPa tidal tweaks  
    • Decades of global catalogues show that even the 3 kPa tide changes total earthquake rate by <2 %.  
    • Teleseismic surface waves (10–50 kPa dynamic stress) sometimes trigger tremor; micro-pascals never do.  
    • If a 10⁻⁶ Pa, phase-tuned cue mattered, we would already see statistically sharp bursts linked to minute-scale lunar phase—none are observed.

7.  No one has tried a multi-site heater “tidal sync” experiment  
    • There are only three large HF heaters left (HAARP, EISCAT, SURA) and they rarely overlap in schedule.  
    • Running them for a full 12- or 24-h tidal cycle would cost tens of thousands of dollars in diesel and is scientifically unjustifiable because the expected ground signal is far below magnetometer and strainmeter resolution.  
    • Proposal reviews at all three facilities would reject such a plan as non-viable on energy grounds alone.

8.  Even a perfect cue cannot beat Coulomb thresholds  
    • Laboratory and numerical studies put the smallest extra shear/normal stress that measurably advances failure at ≈0.1–1 kPa.  
    • A one-billionth-of-tidal cue is still eight to nine orders beneath that floor.  
    • Without crossing at least one patch’s threshold, no cascade can start, so the “distributed det-cord” never lights.

Key take-away  

Synchronising heater pulses to the lunar tide does not turn micro-pascals into kilopascals, cannot overcome the crust’s kPa-scale activation thresholds, and covers too little real estate to matter even if it could.  Every quantitative piece—Love-number tidal physics, downward coupling, fault mechanics, and global seismic statistics—points the same way: the crust is already being “told” exactly when and how hard to flex by the Moon and Sun, and a human HF whisper arrives eleven orders of magnitude too soft to alter the script.
